$comment: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the graphics seam wire contracts (host:canvas / host:gpu / host:asset). The Rust guest SDK (crates/rill-guest) generates its budget constants from this file (build.rs) and locks its wire shapes to it via conformance tests (crates/rill-guest/src/conformance.rs). Downstream host validators (e.g. a platform's host-canvas.ts / host-gpu.ts OP_SPECS and budget tables) MUST read or mirror THIS file — never hand-copy from the Rust source. Field names are the exact JSON keys on the wire; op names are the exact 'op' values. Budgets are the per-call/per-submit caps the HOST enforces (the guest SDK self-limits with the same numbers, but the host stays authoritative and fails closed).

host:canvas.description: Host-mediated 2D drawing surface. Two paths onto the same sealed <Canvas>: 'draw' replays a validated Canvas2D display list; 'present' blits an RGBA8 framebuffer read (bounds-checked) from the guest's own linear memory. Styles are CSS color STRINGS only (no gradient/pattern objects, hence no image/URL reference) and there is deliberately NO readback op.

draw.description: Replay the display list in 'ops' (each entry an op object from this seam's 'ops' table) onto the <Canvas> keyed by canvasId. Unknown/invalid ops are dropped host-side and counted in 'dropped'.

present.description: Blit width*height*4 bytes of straight-alpha row-major RGBA8 (stride = width*4) read from guest linear memory at 'ptr' (bounds-checked host-side). The response resolves only AFTER the host finished reading, so the ack is the guest's backpressure signal. The host additionally byte-budgets present per canvas; over-budget presents fail closed.

canvas.frame.description: Host rAF tick delivered to the guest (drives per-frame draw/present loops).

ops.$comment: Canvas2D display-list op whitelist. 'op' carries the name (camelCase ctx method); the remaining keys are the exact wire fields. All numeric fields must be finite (NaN/Infinity are not legal JSON; the guest SDK drops such ops and fails the whole list loud).

host:gpu.description: Host-mediated GPU surface (stage ③-a: validated command buffer + host-preset pipelines). The guest never touches a real WebGPU/WebGL2 context; it uploads resources for opaque integer handles and submits op lists from the opcode whitelist below. There is deliberately NO COMPILE_SHADER and NO readback of any kind — the seal's isolation is the ABSENCE of those ops. The host validates every op plus the per-submit cost budget and drops an over-budget submit with a reason (shared-hardware TDR containment).

configure.description: Bind a <Canvas> to a gpu mode. Fails closed (ok:false) if the canvas is unknown or already bound to a conflicting context family (2d / present / the other gpu backend).

createResource.description: Upload a resource, returning an opaque host-table handle. Buffers are slice-copied (bounds-checked, capped by budget.maxBufferBytes) from guest linear memory at ptr/len. Textures come ONLY from an app-package assetId (the host:asset resolver discipline) — never a guest URL.

submit.description: Validate + replay a command buffer against the host-preset pipelines bound to canvasId. 'reason' only accompanies ok:false (unknown/unconfigured canvas, over-budget, device lost). If the op list does not end with SUBMIT the host appends one.

presets.$comment: Host-preset pipeline ids for SET_PIPELINE. The guest cannot author shaders in ③-a; adding a preset is a HOST change (audited), never a guest capability.

SOLID_2D.description: Flat-colored triangles from an interleaved [x, y, r, g, b, a] (f32) vertex buffer. No texture, no guest shader.

TEXTURED_2D.description: Textured quad: samples a host-preset texture bind group whose texture came from an assetId (host:asset). Vertex buffer is [x, y, u, v].

ops.$comment: GPU command-buffer opcode whitelist. 'op' carries the UPPER_SNAKE name; the remaining keys are the exact wire fields. All numeric fields must be finite.

budget.$comment: Per-submit cost budget (except maxBufferBytes, which caps one createResource buffer upload). The host recomputes and enforces these; the guest SDK mirrors them so a cooperative guest self-limits before wasting a submit.

costFormula.$comment: How a submit's running cost is accounted, op by op. 'primitives' and 'instances' and 'pixels' accumulate over the whole submit; maxElementsPerDraw / maxInstancesPerDraw are per-DRAW* caps checked against each draw's own count/instances. Non-instanced draws count as instances=1. viewportArea is the current SET_VIEWPORT w*h in device px (the host falls back to the real canvas dimensions when unset).

gpu.deviceLost.description: The GPU device/context was lost (driver TDR or context loss, possibly caused by another app on the shared GPU). A robust guest re-configures and re-uploads its resources.

host:asset.description: Resolve an app-package assetId to decoded RGBA in the guest's OWN linear memory (the ④ pixera path). The host owns resolution + decode: assetId is looked up in the app manifest, gated (same-origin package raster only — never a guest URL, never svg), and the decode is dimension-capped BEFORE the full raster (anti-bomb). The guest never fetches or decodes anything.

info.description: Decoded dimensions of the asset, or a failed call for an unknown/invalid/undecodable id (fail-closed).

blit.description: The host writes the asset's straight-alpha RGBA8 into guest memory at dstPtr (bounds-checked; refused if dstCap < width*height*4). 'written' is the byte count actually written.
